Title: GmMYB4 Positively Regulates Isoflavone Biosynthesis via the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW Module in Soybean

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Jack; NJAU_32 for cloned promoter/CDS assays
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Competitive isoflavone branch of phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Isoflavones/2-hydroxyisoflavanone intermediates leading to genistein- and daidzein-type isoflavones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the committed branch step converting flavanones toward isoflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the isoflavone branch leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/branch-determining step in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of GmIFS2 promotes isoflavone accumulation; its expression is positively associated with higher TIC, GEC, DAC and GLC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmIFS2 was significantly upregulated in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and downregulated in gmmyb4 mutants. 2. GmIFS2 overexpression lines showed significant TIC increases in R2 leaves (1.41–1.52-fold) and R8 seeds (1.36–1.77-fold). 3. GmIFS2 is differentially regulated by the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW module and represents a key downstream output for isoflavone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; overexpression in soybean lines; metabolite profiling of isoflavones; Dual-LUC reporter assay; Y1H with MBW complex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increased in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and GmMAPK6-overexpressing hairy roots; decreased in gmmyb4 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; developmental stages R2 leaves and R8 seeds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmIFS2pro reporter in Dual-LUC assay; CaMV 35S for effector overexpression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 complex (promoter regulation context); GmMYB4 (indirect regulator)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive dietary compounds beneficial to human health
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competitive relationship with anthocyanin biosynthesis branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme target for increasing soybean seed and leaf isoflavone content; could be stacked with positive regulators such as GmMYB4 or GmMAPK6
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The MBW complex suppresses GmIFS2 promoter activity, and GmMYB4 alleviates this suppression by disrupting MBW assembly.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Jack; GWAS panel of 219 soybean accessions; NJAU_32 for cloned CDS/promoters
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds important for human health
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; contains bHLH-binding motif in R3 domain and EAR motif
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Isoflavone biosynthesis; anthocyanin branch repression; broader flavonoid and proanthocyanidin metabolism branch allocation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional regulation through disruption of MBW complex assembly; competitive binding to bHLH partners; positive regulation of isoflavone pathway and suppression of anthocyanin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmIFS2; GmANS3; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8; GmCHR1; GmCHI1; GmIFS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of isoflavone biosynthetic genes including GmPAL1.3, GmC4H, GmCHS8, GmCHR1, GmCHI1, GmIFS2 and GmIFS1; suppresses GmANS3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator identified by GWAS and functional validation; major switch reallocating flux toward isoflavones and away from anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GmMAPK6-mediated transcriptional activation and phosphorylation at Ser39
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with bHLH TFs GmTT8, GmGL3 and GmEGL3; disrupts GmMYB115-GmTT8(-GmTTG1) MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmTT8; GmGL3; GmEGL3; GmMAPK6; GmMAPKK2; GmMYB115 (competitive complex context)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively increases TIC, GEC, DAC and GLC; decreases anthocyanin accumulation; increases seed flavonoids and proanthocyanidins
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified GmMYB4 as the candidate gene at qGlc11 associated with glycitein class content. 2. Overexpression increased TIC, GEC, DAC and GLC in hairy roots, leaves and seeds, while RNAi/knockout reduced them. 3. GmMYB4 upregulated multiple isoflavone-pathway genes and downregulated GmANS3. 4. GmMYB4 interacts with bHLH proteins and disrupts MBW complex formation, relieving repression of GmIFS2 and reducing activation of GmANS3. 5. GmMAPK6 phosphorylates GmMYB4 at Ser39 and positively regulates its expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS/haplotype analysis; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; stable overexpression and CRISPR knockout; RNA-seq; DAP-seq; EMSA; LCI; BiFC; Y2H; Y1H; Dual-LUC; Co-IP; LC-MS/MS phosphorylation assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers, pods and seeds, with highest expression in flowers; altered in OE/RNAi/knockout lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed broadly across soybean tissues, peaking in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field environments for GWAS (Nanjing 2014, 2015, 2018); controlled growth chamber conditions; developmental stages V3, R2 and R8
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for overexpression and localization/effector assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shifts flux away from anthocyanin accumulation; anthocyanin decreased in overexpression leaves
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favorable haplotype GmMYB4H1 and transgenic/editing manipulation provide strong targets for breeding high-isoflavone soybean; can be combined with GmMAPK6 or GmIFS2
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmMYB4 does not directly bind tested promoters of 10 pathway genes by Y1H, indicating indirect control via other regulatory factors; homolog Glyma.12g032200 confers functional compensation in single mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using resequencing SNPs (5,410,112 SNPs); RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000; accession NA); DAP-seq (accession NA)

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots; inferred leaves/seeds through pathway regulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds important for human health
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Upstream signaling control of isoflavone biosynthesis via transcriptional and post-translational regulation of GmMYB4 and pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Phosphorylation activation and positive transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmMYB4; GmPAL1.3; Gm4CL; GmCHI1; GmCHR1; GmIFS2; GmIFS1; GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression upregulates GmMYB4 and multiple isoflavone biosynthetic genes while downregulating GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical upstream MAPK signal connecting kinase cascade to isoflavone accumulation through GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MAPK cascade context; potential input from GmMAPKK2 inferred but not fully established here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression significantly increases isoflavone content and promotes expression of GmMYB4 and isoflavone biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMAPK6 physically interacts with GmMYB4 as shown by BiFC, Co-IP and Y2H using constitutively active kinase. 2. In vitro kinase assay coupled with LC-MS/MS showed that GmMAPK6 phosphorylates GmMYB4 at Ser39. 3. Overexpression of GmMAPK6 in soybean hairy roots significantly increased isoflavone content. 4. GmMAPK6 overexpression upregulated GmMYB4 and several isoflavone biosynthetic genes while suppressing GmANS3.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction prediction plus BiFC; Co-IP; Y2H; constitutively active mutant analysis; in vitro phosphorylation with LC-MS/MS; hairy-root overexpression; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional effects assayed in overexpressing hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hairy-root transformation under standard growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OE construct promoter not explicitly stated for hairy-root overexpression; CaMV 35S used broadly for transient assays in the study
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter competing anthocyanin branch by repressing GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering target to boost isoflavone accumulation, especially in combination with GmMYB4-based strategies; phosphorylation-site-aware engineering may further optimize effect
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper proposes both transcriptional activation of GmMYB4 and post-translational regulation via phosphorylation; in vivo phosphorylation remains to be further validated.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: STRING-based PPI prediction; no accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: NJAU_32 for cloned CDS/promoters; assays in N. benthamiana transient system
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds important for human health
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: MBW-complex-mediated branch selection between isoflavone and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation through MBW complex formation with bHLH and WD-repeat partners
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Promoters of GmANS3; GmOMT5; Gm4CL; GmIFS2; GmANR2; GmF3H1; GmUGT; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Weak promoter binding alone; in combination with GmTT8 forms MBW complex that suppresses GmIFS2 promoter activity and activates GmANS3 promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a direct MBW component controlling competitive branch genes targeted by GmMYB4-mediated interference
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Interfered with by GmMYB4 competition for GmTT8 binding
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complex with GmTT8 and GmTTG1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As MBW component, contributes to repression of isoflavone branch gene GmIFS2 and activation of anthocyanin gene GmANS3; GmMYB4 counteracts this effect
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB115 is a soybean MYB component of the MBW complex with GmTT8 and GmTTG1. 2. GmMYB115 alone weakly binds target promoters, but binding is enhanced by GmTT8. 3. The MBW complex suppresses GmIFS2 and activates GmANS3 in transient assays. 4. GmMYB4 competitively disrupts GmMYB115-GmTT8 complex formation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in N. benthamiana leaves
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for effector constructs; GmIFS2pro and GmANS3pro in Dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotes anthocyanin branch while suppressing isoflavone branch
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for reducing MBW repression of isoflavone synthesis, for example by weakening GmMYB115-GmTT8 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because direct mechanistic experiments in Results established its causal role within the GmMYB4-controlled MBW module.
